commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=83e2b8cffb85586b077cbf6c171c9fee6455a31d)
commands.review_summary:
The branch merges same-source/path keyword and vector results whose line ranges overlap despite differing chunk IDs, adds overlap and disjoint-range tests, and replaces the shared FTS token normalizer with equivalent local processing.

PR surface: Source +44, Tests +71. Total +115 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source with high confidence: differing IDs survive the merge, then lower weighted keyword-only hits are discarded whenever another strict result survives. A real indexed reproduction was not independently run.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers should select one canonical score-preservation approach between the two open PRs and require real indexed-search proof before merge.

target.title: fix(memory-core): preserve overlapping hybrid keyword scores
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes hybrid memory search scoring for cases where FTS keyword results and vector results refer to overlapping memory ranges but use different chunk ids.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: hybrid memory search no longer drops keyword textScore when FTS and vector retrieval identify overlapping chunks with different ids.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/stellarfish/openclaw-matrix-proof`, running the memory-core runtime helper with `node_modules/.bin/tsx --conditions=development` against the patched source.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the OpenClaw memory-core runtime helper with overlapping and disjoint `mergeHybridResults` inputs, then ran the scoped regression and hygiene commands listed in Validation.

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the patched OpenClaw checkout:

```console
$ node_modules/.bin/tsx --conditions=development --eval 'import { mergeHybridResults } from "./extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.ts"; /* invoke overlapping and disjoint hybrid inputs */'
{
  "overlapping": [
    {
      "path": "memory/cjk.md",
      "startLine": 10,
      "endLine": 20,
      "score": 0.8,
      "vectorScore": 0.6,
      "textScore": 1,
      "snippet": "飞书插件配置"
    }
  ],
  "disjointCount": 2,
  "disjointScores": [
    {
      "vectorScore": 0,
      "textScore": 1,
      "snippet": "unrelated keyword"
    },
    {
      "vectorScore": 0.6,
      "textScore": 0,
      "snippet": "vector chunk"
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: the overlapping keyword/vector ranges now merge into one result carrying both component scores and the keyword snippet, while the disjoint same-file keyword range remains separate from the vector hit.

What was not tested: live end-to-end `openclaw memory_search` against a trigram sqlite index with Ollama embeddings.

## Changes

- Merge keyword textScore into same source/path vector results when line ranges overlap and ids differ.
- Keep exact chunk-id matching as the first merge path.
- Leave disjoint same-file keyword results separate to avoid unrelated score blending.
- Add regression coverage for overlapping-id drift and disjoint-range protection.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts -t "preserves keyword-only hybrid hits when minScore exceeds text weight"`
- `pnpm format:check extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts`
- `git diff --check -- extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts`

## Risks

- Does not attempt semantic similarity matching; the fallback is scoped to line overlap only.
target.author: yu-xin-c
